mappings.rationale: Covers ancient Egyptian civilization including its religion, directly relevant to 822.A.3 on Egyptian religion.
Directly addresses Egyptian religious mythology, relevant to 822.A.3.b on religious literature and mythology of Egypt.
Covers Egyptian religious art, symbolism, and iconography, directly relevant to 822.A.3.e on religious symbolism and iconography.
Covers Greek and Roman mythology, directly relevant to 822.C.2 on Greek mythology and 822.D on Roman religion.
Covers mythological traditions of ancient peoples worldwide, relevant to the broad scope of section 822 on religions of ancient peoples.
Covers ancient Greek civilization including its religious practices and institutions, relevant to 822.C on Greek religion.
Covers Roman civilization including its religious practices, imperial cults, and the rise of Christianity, relevant to 822.D on Roman religion.
Covers the Roman Republic's civic and family religion, relevant to 822.D on Roman religion including gods, worship, and institutions.
Covers the study of Greek and Roman civilizations including their religions, relevant to 822.C and 822.D on Greek and Roman religion.
Covers ancient Near Eastern civilizations and their religions, directly relevant to 822.A on religions of ancient Near Eastern peoples.
Covers Assyrian civilization including its Mesopotamian religion, relevant to 822.A.2 on Mesopotamian religions.
Covers Babylonian civilization including its Mesopotamian religion, relevant to 822.A.2 on Mesopotamian religions.
Covers the Hellenistic period's religious developments, directly relevant to 822.E on religions of the Hellenistic world.
Covers the religious transformations of late antiquity including mystery religions, Gnosticism, and the rise of Christianity, relevant to 822.E.
Covers Viking civilization including Norse Germanic religion, relevant to 822.G.2 on religion of the Germanic peoples.
Covers Celtic peoples including their religious beliefs and practices, directly relevant to 822.G.1 on religion of the Celts.
Covers the religious leadership and practices of the Celtic druids, directly relevant to 822.G.1 on religion of the Celts.
Covers pre-Christian polytheistic religions of Europe including Greek, Roman, Celtic, and Germanic traditions, relevant to 822.C, D, F, and G.
Covers Etruscan civilization including its religious practices that influenced Roman religion, relevant to 822.D on Roman religion.
Covers the mythological and archaeological context of the Trojan War including its religious dimensions, relevant to 822.C.2 on Greek mythology.
Covers Epicurean philosophy as a Hellenistic religious philosophy, directly relevant to 822.E.4 on Hellenistic religious philosophies.
Covers Stoic philosophy as a Hellenistic religious philosophy, directly relevant to 822.E.4 on stoicism as a Hellenistic religious philosophy.
Covers ancient Greek and Roman philosophy including its religious dimensions and Hellenistic philosophical schools, relevant to 822.E.4.
Covers early Greek philosophical thought about the divine and the cosmos, relevant to 822.C.3 on Greek religious beliefs and speculation.
Plato's philosophy has significant religious dimensions including views on the divine, the soul, and the afterlife, relevant to 822.C.3 and 822.E.4.
Aristotle's theology and metaphysics influenced later religious philosophy, relevant to 822.C.3 on Greek religious beliefs and speculation.
Covers archaeological evidence for ancient Near Eastern and Syrian-Palestinian religions, relevant to 822.A.5 on Syrian and Palestinian religions.
Covers Aztec civilization and its elaborate religious system of sacrifice, temples, and cosmology, closely related to the pre-Columbian American religions covered in 822.I.
Covers Maya civilization including its religion, directly relevant to 822.I.2 on Mayan religion.
Covers the encounter between European and pre-Columbian religions during the conquest, providing context for 822.I on religions of pre-Columbian American civilizations.
Alexander's conquests shaped the Hellenistic religious world including the spread of Greek religion and syncretism, relevant to 822.E on religions of the Hellenistic world.
Covers Byzantine civilization including the late continuation of Hellenistic religious traditions and early Christianity, relevant to 822.E.
Covers Spartan civilization including its distinctive religious practices and institutions, relevant to 822.C.4 on Greek worship, practices, and institutions.
Homer's epics are foundational texts for Greek religion and mythology, directly relevant to 822.C.2 on Greek mythology and religious literature.
Herodotus is a primary source for ancient Near Eastern, Egyptian, and Greek religious practices, relevant to 822.A and 822.C.
Thucydides documents Greek religious practices and institutions in the context of the Peloponnesian War, relevant to 822.C.4.
Covers Egyptian hieroglyphic writing which was deeply tied to Egyptian religion and sacred texts, relevant to 822.A.3.b on Egyptian religious literature.
Covers Greek and Roman literary works that are primary sources for understanding ancient religion, mythology, and ritual practice.
Sophocles' tragedies engage deeply with Greek religious themes, the gods, and ritual, relevant to 822.C.2 on Greek mythology and religious literature.
Ovid's Metamorphoses and Fasti are key sources for Greek and Roman mythology and religious festivals, relevant to 822.C.2 and 822.D.
Horace's poetry engages with Roman religion, gods, and religious practice, relevant to 822.D on Roman religion.
Provides context for the religious history of central European peoples, tangentially relevant to 822.F on religions of early peoples of eastern and central Europe.
The Crusades involved encounters between Christianity and religions of the Near East, relevant to 822.A on religions of the ancient Near Eastern peoples in their historical context.
Covers Iranian civilization including its pre-Islamic religious heritage (Zoroastrianism), relevant to 822.B on religions of the Iranian peoples.
